meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: SDA Director Drops ‘Acting’ Label and Adds All Missile Warning to Portfolio
immediate.summary: After eight months leading the Space Development Agency on an acting basis, Gurpartap “GP” Sandhoo was named SDA’s permanent leader and given a second hat as the Space Force’s portfolio acquisition executive for missile warning and tracking programs, the service announced May 19. The post SDA Director Drops ‘Acting’ Label and Adds All Missile Warning to Portfolio appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Pentagon inks $500 million deal with Perennial Autonomy for counter-drone tech
immediate.summary: The Pentagon awarded Perennial Autonomy a $500 million contract to accelerate procurement of counter-drone technology.

immediate.title: Space Force Picks Northrop to Build Enhanced Jam-Resistent SATCOM Prototype
immediate.summary: The Space Force awarded Northrop Grumman a $398 million contract to design and build a communications satellite prototype with advanced anti-jam and data processing capabilities. The service announced the contract for the Enhanced Protected Tactical SATCOM-Prototype program, or Enhanced PTS-P, May 15, and said the satellite will launch no sooner than fiscal 2030. The post Space Force Picks Northrop to Build Enhanced Jam-Resistent SATCOM Prototype appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Air Force Raises Bar for Religious Waiver Requests, Reduces Chaplains’ Input
immediate.summary: The Department of the Air Force issues a new policy on requests for religious accommodation, raising the bar for such requests as it implements guidance from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. The new policy emphasizes the role of commanders while limiting that of chaplains. It also eliminates special boards designed to advise leaders. The post Air Force Raises Bar for Religious Waiver Requests, Reduces Chaplains Input appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P
immediate.summary: The Space Forces Space Systems Command (SSC) has awarded Northrop Grumman [NOC] a $398 million firm-fixed price contract to develop and build an Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications-Prototype (PTS-P) for [] The post Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Space Force names Sandhoo as head of new missile warning/tracking PAE
immediate.summary: As of May 11, Sandhoo is double hatted as both head of the PAE and director of the Space Development Agency.

immediate.title: New Air-to-Air Missile Photographed in Testing at Eglin
immediate.summary: The military’s secretive new air-to-air missile appears to have been publicly photographed for the first time during testing. A missile matching previously released information and renders of the AIM-260 Joint Advanced Tactical Missile, or JATM, was photographed being carried by an F/A-18F Super Hornet on May 13, shortly after taking off from Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. The post New Air-to-Air Missile Photographed in Testing at Eglin appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services
immediate.summary: Skynopy has won a new contract from U-Space, a Toulouse-based next-generation small satellite manufacturer. Skynopy will be U-Space’s ground station service provider for the operation of its two satellites in [] The post Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Aeralis enters administration blaming defense-spending delays
immediate.summary: Aeralis falls into administration after delays to UK defense-funding and the loss of overseas backing Continue reading Aeralis enters administration blaming defense-spending delays at Aerospace Testing International.

immediate.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Spies Mars’ Wind-Blown Craters During Close Approach
immediate.summary: Description This view of the Martian surface, captured by NASA’s Psyche spacecraft on May 15, 2026, shows streaks that have formed due to wind blowing over impact craters in the Syrtis Major region. The image scale is nearly 1,200 feet (360 meters) per pixel. The wind streaks extend to about 30 miles (50 kilometers) long, […]

immediate.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Sees Mars’ South Pole After Flyby
immediate.summary: Description This is Psyche’s first view of a nearly “full Mars” seen shortly after the spacecraft’s closest approach to the planet on May 15, 2026. The view extends from the south polar cap northwards to the Valles Marineris canyon system and beyond. With Mars in the rearview mirror, the spacecraft will soon resume use of […]

immediate.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images the Crescent of Mars
immediate.summary: Description This view of a crescent Mars was captured on May 15, 2026, at about 5:03 a.m. PDT by NASA’s Psyche mission as it approached the planet for a gravity assist. Captured by the spacecraft’s multispectral imager instrument, this was the last view of the whole planet before it began to overfill the field of […]

immediate.title: Air Force Pauses All T-38 Trainer Flights, a Week After Alabama Crash
immediate.summary: The Air Force on May 19 announced it has temporarily halted all T-38 Talon flights, a week after one of the decades-old trainer jets crashed. The post Air Force Pauses All T-38 Trainer Flights, a Week After Alabama Crash appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Aces Mars Flyby, Targets Metal-Rich Asteroid
immediate.summary: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft completed its close approach of Mars on May 15, coming within 2,864 miles (4,609 kilometers) of the planet’s surface. This flyby used a gravity assist from Mars to provide a critical boost in speed and to adjust the spacecraft’s orbital plane without using any onboard propellant, sending it on its way toward []

immediate.title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' clip reveals how Mando gets his Razor Crest spaceship back (video)
immediate.summary: 'Well, let’s call it an advance on the next mission.'

immediate.title: Commercial Space Station Builder Vast Expands Into Satellite Buses
immediate.summary: Vast, a company known for developing the Haven commercial space stations, is now diversifying its business with a move into high-power satellite buses. Vast announced the expansion on Tuesday, along [] The post Commercial Space Station Builder Vast Expands Into Satellite Buses appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
immediate.summary: The commercial space station company announced this morning that it would start selling satellite buses, specifically targeting the low-cost, high-volume, high-power niche for missions from comms, to EO, to national-security programs. The post Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: Air Force Special Ops Pushes Forward with ‘Swiss Army Knife’ OA-1K
immediate.summary: With a shrinking fleet and growing operational demand, Air Force Special Operations Command sees the new OA-1K combat scout aircraft as key to a “new era,” officials say. The post Air Force Special Ops Pushes Forward with ‘Swiss Army Knife’ OA-1K appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Sweden selects French FDI frigates from Naval Group
immediate.summary: “We have seen that Russia has increased its production of ballistic missiles over the past year. Our capability in this area [air defence] will triple with this acquisition,” Defense Minister Pål Jonson said aboard the navy corvette today.

immediate.title: The UFO files: What did we learn from the Pentagon's 1st big release?
immediate.summary: On May 8, the Pentagon released its first tranche of "UFO files." Space.com reached out to experts to make sense of this highly anticipated data drop.

immediate.title: Two Navy EA-18G Growlers Collide at Air Force Base Air Show
immediate.summary: Two Navy EA-18G Growlers collided midair on May 17 during an air show at Mountain Home Air Force Base, Idaho. All four crew members ejected safely, and there were no fatalities. The post Two Navy EA-18G Growlers Collide at Air Force Base Air Show appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: What time is SpaceX's Starship V3 launch on May 21? (Starship Flight 12 timeline)
immediate.summary: SpaceX will launch its first Starship V3 vehicle on the Flight 12 test flight on Thursday (May 21).

immediate.title: Vega C rocket launches European-Chinese space weather satellite to orbit
immediate.summary: A Vega C rocket launched the European-Chinese SMILE space weather mission to orbit on Monday night (May 18).

immediate.title: US Air Force looks to convert offshore oil rigs into rocket recovery platforms
immediate.summary: An Air Force plan calls for old oil platforms to become Sea-based Recovery Stations for the U.S. Space Force and private spaceflight companies.

immediate.title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12 launch updates: 1st Starship V3 will now launch on May 21
immediate.summary: SpaceX is targeting a Wednesday, May 20, launch for its newest Starship design, the Starship V3 megarocket.

immediate.title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
immediate.summary: Skynopy’s ground-station-as-a-service model is quickly gaining traction. The post U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: Giant radio telescope sees Artemis 2 astronauts on Orion flying around the moon. 'There are 4 people in those pixels.'
immediate.summary: A giant radio telescope in West Virginia tracked Artemis 2's Orion spacecraft around the moon for five days, gathering precise observations on its movements during the historic mission.

immediate.title: Lasers in moon craters could create a lunar GPS system
immediate.summary: Scientists are exploring how lasers stabilized in the moon's coldest, darkest craters could enable a GPS-like network to help future Artemis astronauts and spacecraft navigate the lunar surface with far greater precision.

immediate.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Mars’ Huygens Crater
immediate.summary: Description Captured by the multispectral imager instrument on NASA’s Psyche mission, this is an enhanced-color view of the large double-ring crater Huygens (upper left; about 290 miles, or 470 kilometers, in diameter) and the surrounding heavily cratered southern highlands near 15 degrees south latitude. The various colors in this dramatic scene are likely due to […]

immediate.title: Psyche’s High-Resolution View of Mars’ South Pole
immediate.summary: Description This is the highest-resolution view of the water ice-rich south polar cap of Mars captured by NASA’s Psyche mission after it made its close approach with the planet for a gravity assist. The image scale is around 0.7 miles per pixel (1.14 kilometers per pixel). The cap itself extends across more than 430 miles […]

immediate.title: Worker dies at SpaceX's Starbase in leadup to Starship V3 megarocket launch
immediate.summary: A worker died at SpaceX's Starbase site in South Texas early Friday morning (May 15), as the company geared up for the debut launch of its Starship V3 megarocket.

immediate.title: Italy Wil Procure Six A330 MRTT Tankers to Replace KC-767
immediate.summary: Documents show that Italy has signed in April 2026 a deal to acquire six A330 MRTT tankers which will replace the current fleet of four KC-767s. Italy has reportedly selected new tanker aircraft for the Italian Air Force which will replace the current fleet of KC-767 tankers. According to the European Union’s Tenders Electronic Daily []

immediate.title: Sky Perfect JSAT Invests in Astroscale to Partner on On-Orbit Servicing
immediate.summary: Sky Perfect JSAT is investing in Astroscale as part of a strategic partnership between the companies to work together on on-orbit servicing initiatives. Through the partnership announced Tuesday, the companies [] The post Sky Perfect JSAT Invests in Astroscale to Partner on On-Orbit Servicing appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: Breaking the Power Constraint in Space With Star Catcher’s Andrew Rush
immediate.summary: SWAP — size, weight, and power — is a defining feature of space engineering constraints. As new technologies and designs have opened up options for size and weight, power is [] The post Breaking the Power Constraint in Space With Star Catcher’s Andrew Rush appeared first on Via Satellite .

immediate.title: MQ-25A Stingray cleared for deployment, says Cao
immediate.summary: The role of the MQ-25A is intended to provide the Carrier Air Wing with unmanned refueling, freeing up F/A-18E/F aircraft to focus on strike missions.

immediate.title: Vast, builder of private space stations, launches line of high-power satellites
immediate.summary: The California startup Vast, which is best known for its space station plans, announced today (May 19) that it's launching a new line of high-power satellites.

immediate.title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
immediate.summary: Depending on which scenario comes to fruition, the report predicts the space community will want to launch between 6,000 and 230,000 sats per year—requiring up to 7,000 launches annually, according to a release. The post CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low appeared first on Payload .

immediate.title: NASA satellite will test orbital 'gas station' tech to help astronauts reach the moon and Mars
immediate.summary: The Liquid Oxygen Flight demonstration (LOXSAT) will help NASA test and develop technologies to support in-space transfer and storage of cryogenic propellants, critical abilities for the Artemis program to return astronauts to the moon.

same_day.title: Polish officials vent worries over scrapped US troop deployment
same_day.summary: “More than fifty billion dollars is the sum of the purchases that we are implementing in the United States,” Poland's defense minister noted.

same_day.title: SpaceX Starlink and other satellite megaconstellations are creating an 'unregulated geoengineering experiment', scientists say
same_day.summary: The space industry's plans to launch millions of satellites worry scientists as the rise in high-altitude air pollution threatens to alter Earth's climate.

same_day.summary: Also partners with Rooverse on securing human-generated social data in space

same_day.title: Johnson’s Cindy Evans Prepares Artemis Teams for Lunar Science
same_day.summary: NASA’s Artemis II crew had many technical and operational responsibilities during their historic mission to the Moon, but they also served an important role as scientific ambassadors to Earth’s nearest neighbor. On their 10-day journey, the crew flew by the far side of the Moon, analyzing and photographing geologic features such as impact craters and []

same_day.title: Galactic starlight will take your breath away | Space photo of the day for May 19, 2026
same_day.summary: The galaxy M77 looks truly out-of-this-world in a new image by the James Webb Space Telescope.

same_day.title: NASA’s New Shock Detectives Project Invites Volunteers to Help Study Solar Wind
same_day.summary: NASA’s Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission has collected more than ten years of data from this zone – more than scientists can analyze alone. As Shock Detectives, you’ll help sort the chaotic from peaceful regions of the data, giving researchers a crucial set of clues.

same_day.title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
same_day.summary: York Space Systems’ ($YSS) MA team is having a busy year. The post York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial appeared first on Payload .

same_day.summary: Romanian F-16s deployed as part of the Baltic Air Policing shot down a rogue drone, possibly of Ukrainian origin, which entered Estonian airspace from Russia. The Estonian Defense Forces have announced that Romanian F-16 Fighting Falcons have shot down a drone which entered Estonian airspace. Estonia says that the drone, which arrived from Russian airspace, []

same_day.title: NATO jet shoots down Ukrainian drone over Estonia in escalation of airspace violations
same_day.summary: The drone was shot down by a Romanian F-16 fighter jet stationed in Šiauliai, Lithuania, Estonian media reported.

same_day.title: Scientists just solved a tricky asteroid-hopping spacecraft riddle
same_day.summary: Plotting the optimal trajectory to visit multiple asteroids is a fiendishly difficult take on the Traveling Salesperson problem, but a new mathematical approach has succeeded in solving it.

same_day.summary: [The] Lethality Challenge selection gives us a rail-locked pathway to thousands or tens of thousands of unit orders for this product, which has absolutely changed the caliber of discussion we’re having with investors, suppliers, other customers/partners, etc, drone-maker Bravos Kevin Landtroop told Breaking Defense.

same_day.summary: A new NASA video shows the Artemis 2 Orion capsule's separation from its service module just before the spacecraft slammed into Earth's atmosphere on April 10, 2026.

same_day.title: ISIS leader killed in Africa as US commander raises force reduction concerns
